profile.name: Mohamed Aymen Akchiche
profile.title: ML & Generative AI Engineer
profile.bio: Computer Science Engineering student focused on Machine Learning, NLP, and Generative AI systems. Passionate about building intelligent applications powered by data and modern AI technologies.

projects.title: Spam classification
projects.description: A machine learning project that automatically classifies email and text messages as Spam or Not Spam (Ham). The system combines Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques with a Naive Bayes classifier to detect unwanted messages and improve communication security.
projects.overview: The Spam Detection System is a binary text classification application designed to identify spam messages based on their textual content. The project utilizes TF-IDF (Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency) to convert text into numerical features that capture the importance of words within messages. These features are then used by a Naive Bayes model to accurately distinguish between legitimate and spam communications.
projects.problem: Spam messages continue to be a major challenge in digital communication, causing security concerns, reduced productivity, and poor user experience. Building an effective spam filter requires accurately identifying unwanted messages while avoiding the incorrect classification of legitimate communications. Additionally, imbalanced datasets can negatively impact model performance and lead to biased predictions.
projects.approach: The project begins with text preprocessing, including data cleaning, tokenization, and the removal of unnecessary characters. The textual data is then transformed into numerical representations using TF-IDF vectorization, which highlights important terms while reducing the influence of commonly occurring words. During exploratory analysis, the dataset was found to be imbalanced, so balancing techniques were applied to improve the model's ability to learn from both classes effectively. A Naive Bayes classifier was then trained on the TF-IDF features to perform binary classification. Model performance was evaluated using Accuracy (overall prediction correctness), Precision (the proportion of predicted spam messages that were actually spam), and Recall (the proportion of actual spam messages successfully identified). The final model achieved strong results across all evaluation metrics, demonstrating its effectiveness in detecting spam while minimizing classification errors.

projects.title: Email generator
projects.description: An AI-powered email generation tool that creates professional and personalized emails based on user-defined requirements. The application leverages LangChain and the Gemini API to generate complete emails, including both the subject line and body, while adapting to the desired style, tone, emotion, and communication context.
projects.overview: The Email Generator is a Generative AI application designed to simplify email writing by transforming user instructions into well-structured and context-aware emails. Users can provide details such as the sender's name, email purpose, target audience, writing style, emotional tone, and key points to include. The system then generates a polished email with an appropriate subject line, helping users save time while maintaining effective communication.
projects.problem: Writing professional emails can be time-consuming, especially when users need to adapt their message to different contexts, audiences, and tones. Many individuals struggle to find the right wording or structure for formal, persuasive, friendly, or emotionally sensitive communications. This challenge becomes more significant when frequent email creation is required, leading to reduced productivity and inconsistent messaging.
projects.approach: The project utilizes LangChain as the orchestration framework and the Gemini API as the underlying large language model. Users provide customization parameters such as the sender's name, email context, target audience, writing style, tone, emotion, and additional instructions. These inputs are incorporated into a carefully designed prompt that guides the model to generate a coherent and contextually appropriate email. The system then produces both a relevant subject line and a complete email body tailored to the user's requirements, ensuring consistency, clarity, and personalization.

projects.title: Customer Churn Prediction
projects.description: A machine learning project that predicts customer churn in the banking sector and segments customers into distinct groups based on their behavioral and demographic characteristics. The system combines predictive analytics, customer segmentation, and generative AI-powered recommendations to help banks identify at-risk customers and improve retention strategies.
projects.overview: The Customer Churn Prediction System is designed to analyze customer data and determine the likelihood of a customer leaving the bank. In addition to churn prediction, the project uses clustering techniques to segment customers into meaningful groups, enabling more targeted retention strategies. To enhance decision-making, a recommendation module built with LangChain and the Gemini API generates actionable insights and personalized recommendations based on the prediction and segmentation results.
projects.problem: Customer churn represents a significant challenge for banks, as acquiring new customers is often more expensive than retaining existing ones. Identifying customers who are likely to leave and understanding the characteristics of different customer segments are essential for developing effective retention strategies. Traditional analysis methods may fail to uncover complex patterns in customer behavior, making it difficult to proactively address churn risks.
projects.approach: The project follows a complete data science workflow, starting with data preprocessing, exploratory data analysis, and feature engineering to prepare customer data for modeling. A Random Forest Classifier was trained to predict customer churn and achieved strong performance due to its ability to capture complex relationships within the data. Model effectiveness was evaluated using Accuracy (overall prediction correctness), Precision (how many predicted churners actually churned), and Recall (how many actual churners were successfully identified). For customer segmentation, K-Means clustering was applied, and the optimal number of clusters was selected using the Silhouette Score, which measures how well customers fit within their assigned clusters. Finally, LangChain integrated with the Gemini API was used to generate personalized recommendations and retention strategies based on each customer's predicted churn risk and segment profile.

projects.title: ROP Prediction System
projects.description: A freelance machine learning project developed for the oil and gas industry to predict the Rate of Penetration (ROP) during drilling operations. The system leverages historical drilling and geological data to estimate drilling performance, helping engineers optimize operational parameters and improve drilling efficiency.
projects.overview: The ROP Prediction System is a predictive analytics solution designed to forecast drilling penetration rates based on operational and formation-related parameters. By applying machine learning techniques to drilling data, the system provides accurate ROP estimates that can support decision-making, reduce drilling time, and improve overall well performance.
projects.problem: ate of Penetration is one of the most important indicators of drilling efficiency. However, ROP is influenced by numerous interacting factors, including drilling parameters, mud properties, and geological formations. Predicting ROP accurately using traditional empirical methods is challenging, often resulting in suboptimal drilling decisions, increased costs, and reduced operational efficiency.
projects.approach: The project began with data preprocessing and exploratory analysis, followed by feature engineering to create meaningful variables from drilling parameters and improve predictive performance. Several regression models were then trained and compared, including linear and tree-based approaches. After evaluation, Random Forest Regressor achieved the best results and was further optimized through hyperparameter tuning. Model performance was assessed using R² Score (measures how well the model explains variations in ROP), MAE (average absolute prediction error in ROP units), and RMSE (penalizes large prediction errors and reflects the typical prediction deviation). The final model was integrated into an interactive application for real-time ROP prediction.

projects.title: House Price Estimator
projects.description: A machine learning project that predicts house prices based on property features . The prediction model is built using Linear Regression implemented from scratch using Python, without relying on machine learning libraries, providing a deeper understanding of the underlying mathematics and optimization process.
projects.overview: House Price Estimator is a machine learning project that predicts the price of a house using its features, such as area, number of bedrooms, bathrooms, and other relevant attributes. The project implements a Linear Regression model from scratch in Python to demonstrate the fundamental concepts behind predictive modeling without relying on machine learning frameworks.
projects.problem: Estimating house prices accurately is important for buyers, sellers, and real estate agencies. Manual valuation can be subjective and time-consuming, making it difficult to determine a fair market price based on multiple property characteristics.
projects.approach: The project follows a complete machine learning workflow, starting with data preprocessing and feature selection to prepare the housing data for training. A Linear Regression model is then implemented entirely from scratch using Python and NumPy, with gradient descent used to learn the optimal parameters by minimizing prediction error. After training, the model is evaluated using standard regression metrics such as Mean Squared Error (MSE) and R² Score, and is finally used to predict house prices based on the features of unseen properties.
